---
title: The big picture
subtitle: What BDM is, in one sitting
aliases: [/explanations/index.html]
---

BDM is a framework for organizing all the data a cognitive-science experiment
produces — behavior, questionnaires, physiological signals — so that datasets
are consistent across studies, clear to humans, and readily usable by machines.

This page gives you the whole picture. The deep dives are one click away.

## Three kinds of information

A dataset answers three different kinds of question, and BDM keeps them
distinct and linked:

1. **What happened** — *records*: events (key presses, screen changes, answers
   given) and timeseries (EEG, eye tracking), timestamped as they occurred.
2. **What it means** — *interpretations*: trials. A trial is not observed; it
   is constructed from records by an explicit rule (a *task-pattern*). The
   same event stream under a different rule yields different trials — so BDM
   stores the rule with the data.
3. **What it tells us about people** — *inferences*: models of agents, fit on
   trials or directly on records.

Most formats flatten these into one table and lose the distinctions. Keeping
them separate is what makes BDM datasets auditable: you can always trace an
inference back to the interpretation it relied on, and an interpretation back
to the raw record.

## The anatomy of a dataset

A BDM dataset is a folder with a predictable shape:

```
my-study/
├─ dataset.yaml        # the dataset card: what this is, who, how, license
├─ events/             # records: what happened, as it happened
├─ trials/             # interpretations: tidy tables, one row per trial
│  ├─ response.csv
│  └─ stimulus.csv
└─ models/             # inferences: descriptions of agents
```

Every table is tidy; every name follows the [naming
conventions](/spec/conventions/naming.html); every column is defined in the
[specification](/spec/index.html). You always know where things are and what
they are called.

## Where everything fits

- The **conventions** (naming, indexing, units, folder structure) are rules —
  they live in the [Specification](/spec/index.html).
- The **why** behind the design lives here in Concepts:
  [design process](design-process.qmd) ·
  [the nature of behavioral data](behavioral-data.qmd) ·
  [metadata](metadata.qmd).
- The machine-readable schemas live in the
  [schemas repository](https://behaverse.org/schemas) — this site renders
  summaries of them and never forks them.

## Next steps

Ready to try it? [Get started](/guides/1-getting-started.qmd). Evaluating
whether BDM is right for you? [Why BDM](/why/index.qmd).
